steps.title: Use the table values
steps.content: The table gives $(0,5)$, $(1,\frac{5}{2})$, $(2,\frac{5}{4})$, and $(3,\frac{5}{8})$. Compare how the output changes each time $x$ increases by $1$.

steps.title: Find the exponential pattern
steps.content: Because each $y$-value is half the previous $y$-value, this is exponential decay with starting value $5$ and common factor $\frac{1}{2}$.

steps.title: Write and match the model
steps.content:
The model is:
$$f(x)=5\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^x$$
Since $\frac{1}{2}=2^{-1}$, this is also:
$$f(x)=5\cdot2^{-x}$$
That matches choice D, so D is correct.
